reasoning: The finding is a best-practice gap, not an exploitable vulnerability. Three layered controls prevent meaningful exploitation: (1) admin_url() anchors the redirect to the site's own admin domain, making an off-site open redirect impossible regardless of post_type content; (2) esc_url_raw() is applied to the fully assembled URL before wp_redirect(), encoding any dangerous characters; (3) $post->post_type is read from the wp_posts table via get_post(), so an attacker must already have write access to the database to influence this value — a precondition that exceeds the severity of the claimed vulnerability. The auth chain (nonce + edit_post capability check in dt_duplicate_post_as_draft) correctly gates access. Missing sanitize_key() on a DB-sourced field wrapped by esc_url_raw() and prefixed by admin_url() does not constitute a reachable attack path. The finding should be downgraded to informational / defense-in-depth recommendation.
